# Starithm

> Real-time astronomical event intelligence. Starithm ingests multi-messenger
> alerts from NASA's GCN (General Coordinates Network) — gamma-ray bursts,
> gravitational waves, neutrinos, fast radio bursts and X-ray transients — and
> aggregates the streaming GCN Notices and follow-up GCN Circulars for each event
> into a single canonical record with an AI-generated summary.

Starithm (https://starithm.ai) is a platform for astronomers. Every astronomical
event has one page combining its real-time GCN notices, the human-written GCN
Circulars that follow, sky position, and an AI summary, marked up with Schema.org
`NewsArticle` + `Dataset` metadata and a ready-to-use citation. Event and blog
pages are prerendered server-side, so their full content is available to agents
without executing JavaScript.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://starithm.ai/): platform overview.
- [Events](https://starithm.ai/novatrace/events): live list of astronomical events (GRBs, gravitational waves, neutrinos, FRBs, X-ray transients).
- [Event detail](https://starithm.ai/novatrace/events/GBM_794811435): a single event — AI summary, sky position (RA/Dec, J2000), significance, GCN notice and circular counts, source instruments, and a BibTeX citation. URL pattern: `https://starithm.ai/novatrace/events/{canonicalId}`.
- [Blog](https://starithm.ai/blog): explainers on new events and recent astrophysics papers.
- [Roadmap](https://starithm.ai/blog/roadmap): what Starithm is building next.

## Data and feeds

- [Sitemap](https://starithm.ai/sitemap.xml): all event and blog URLs.
- [RSS feed](https://starithm.ai/feed.xml): latest blog posts.
- [robots.txt](https://starithm.ai/robots.txt): crawler policy — AI agents are welcome.
- [llms-full.txt](https://starithm.ai/llms-full.txt): a fuller, single-file description of the platform, its data model, and how to cite it.

## About

Starithm sources alerts from the [GCN](https://gcn.nasa.gov) real-time Kafka
streams and the GCN Circulars archive. Event names follow standard designations
(e.g. `GRB 260714B`, `IceCube-260712A`, `S250303ay`). Content is provided for
research and educational use; please cite the event page (BibTeX is shown on each
page) alongside the original GCN notices and circulars.
